title: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei

herb_names_common.name: esteve's rock rose (helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei)

what_description: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a compact, evergreen shrub with a spreading habit, reaching up to 30 cm in height and 40 cm in width, with ovate leaves that are 1-2 cm long and have a glaucous, slightly hairy surface. The leaves are opposite, simple, and have entire margins. The plant produces small, white to pinkish flowers in axillary clusters, with five petals that are 8-12 mm long and have a five-lobed calyx. The flowers are borne from April to July and are followed by small, red or purple follicles.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei are inflammation and wound healing, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. This plant has also been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in skin irritation and fungal infections, such as athlete's foot, by reducing redness and promoting wound healing. Additionally, Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei has been studied for its effects on digestive issues, including diarrhea and gastroenteritis, as it has been shown to have antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties that can help to reduce bacterial loads in the gut. Furthermore, the plant's antioxidant properties have been found to help reduce fever, hypertension, and pain, which may also contribute to its potential to help manage diabetes, arthritis, and rheumatism. Its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties have also been found to help reduce bacterial infections, cough, eczema, and stomach ulcers, while its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may also help to prevent cancer.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei are attributed to its antioxidant capacity, which is largely due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, allowing it to neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. This plant exhibits antimicrobial properties, primarily mediated by saponins and volatile oils, which inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and prevent infections. Additionally, it displays anti-inflammatory activity, mainly attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Furthermore, Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei has been found to exhibit analgesic properties, likely due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which modulate pain perception and provide relief from discomfort. Its cardioprotective effects are also notable, mainly attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which improve cardiovascular health and protect against heart disease.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei are flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, while phenolic acids like caffeic acid exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, rosmarinic acid has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and tannins are known for their astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. Iridoid glycosides such as aucubin have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-spasmodic activities. Studies have also shown that the combination of these constituents in Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions, including inflammation, infections, and digestive issues. Overall, the bioactive properties of these constituents make Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei a valuable plant for the development of new therapeutic agents.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei are made from its flowers and leaves to extract its bioactive compounds. A tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant, typically made by soaking the flowers and leaves in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin. An infusion is a liquid extract made by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, and can be consumed as a tea, which is a more diluted form of the infusion. A salve is a topical preparation made by infusing the flowers and leaves in a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, to extract their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. A decoction is a concentrated liquid extract made by boiling the dried flowers and leaves in water to extract their bioactive compounds, which can be used as a topical application or internal remedy.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei improperly are experiencing allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and allergic reactions, due to the plant's sap containing allergenic compounds like terpenes and flavonoids. This can lead to symptoms like redness, itching, swelling, and blistering on the skin. In severe cases, an allergic reaction can cause nausea, vomiting, and difficulty breathing. The improper use of Helianthemum apenninum subsp. estevei can also trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. This is why it is crucial to handle and use the plant with caution, under the guidance of a medical professional.
